title: Rich Roasted Polish Pork Casseroles
description: A rich and juicy solo-dinner Polish dish featuring beer and candy, with a macrobiotic-friendly twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, combine the pork shoulder, dark beer, brown sugar, caraway seeds, and marjoram.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the pork for 2 hours, or until it is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
While the pork is roasting, prepare the caramelized onions by cooking them in a pan with a little oil over low heat for 30 minutes.
After the pork has roasted for 2 hours, add the sliced potatoes to the pot and continue to roast for another 30 minutes.
Stir in the sour cream and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
To serve, shred the pork and top it with the caramelized onions, sour cream, and chopped parsley.
